input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the vehicle on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the vehicle on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule T.7.6.3 (which refers to T.7.3.3) specified in the FSAE rule document? Only use dimensions explicitly shown in the engineering drawing to answer the question. If a dimension is not explicitly shown, you can assume that it complies with the rules. First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.7.6.3
            TITLE: when rearward of the centerline of the rear wheel axles:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.6.3: When rearward of the centerline of the rear wheel axles:
T.7.6.3 When rearward of the centerline of the rear wheel axles: 
In the Rear Aerodynamic Zone

RULE: t.7.3.3
            TITLE: rear aerodynamic zone

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.3.3: Rear Aerodynamic Zone
T.7.3.3 Rear Aerodynamic Zone 
The volume that is: 
• Rearward of the Head Restraint Plane 
• Inboard of two vertical planes parallel to the centerline of the chassis touching the inside 
of the rear tires at the height of the hub centerline

final_answer:
Explanation:The rule T.7.3.3 defines the Rear Aerodynamic Zone based on three criteria: being rearward of the Head Restraint Plane, and being laterally contained by planes touching the inside of the rear tires at the hub centerline height. The engineering drawing provides overall dimensions (e.g., 23.79 mm) and some smaller dimensions (0.4 and 0.35). However, the drawing does not explicitly provide the location of the Head Restraint Plane relative to the rear axle centerline, nor does it provide the specific dimensions required to verify the lateral boundaries defined by the inside of the rear tires at the hub centerline height. Following the instruction to assume compliance if a dimension is not explicitly shown, and since no explicit dimension contradicts the rule, the design is deemed compliant based on the provided data.

Answer: Yes
